description: secretx-wolfhsm/src/lib.rs:169 backend_err() unconditionally maps every wolfhsm::Error to SecretError::Backend (permanent). wolfhsm error taxonomy includes transient codes that callers can retry: WH_ERROR_NOTREADY (-2001), WH_ERROR_ABORTED (-2002), WH_ERROR_TIMEOUT (-2010), WH_ERROR_REQUEST_PENDING (-2011), WH_ERROR_LOCKED (-2100), WH_SHE_ERC_BUSY (-2209), and Error::Ffi (transport-level failures from the C wolfssl/wolfcrypt layer). Trigger: wolfHSM server temporarily busy or under load -> nvm_list / nvm_metadata / nvm_read / nvm_overwrite / nvm_add returns one of these codes -> get()/put() returns SecretError::Backend, telling the caller's retry policy 'do not retry' even though a retry would succeed. Same anti-pattern flagged P0 on aws-kms (crate-secretsx-p9bo), pkcs11 (crate-secretsx-nde2.2), azure-kv (crate-secretsx-bdu0.2). Fix: introduce a classify(wolfhsm::Error) -> SecretError that maps the transient codes above to Unavailable and the rest to Backend.
title: Transient wolfhsm errors mapped to permanent SecretError::Backend instead of Unavailable
